summary: Explosive third set with The Other One, Truckin' > Eyes jam and tight playing
blurb: The UCSB show builds a solid first set and erupts in a thunder‑thick third set highlighted by a monster The Other One and the iconic Truckin' → The Other One → Eyes of the World sequence. Jerry’s raspy vocals cut through despite a cold, while Keith and Donna add soaring harmonies. Overall it’s a high‑energy ’73 classic that rewards repeated listening.
review: May 20, 1973 at the UC Santa Barbara Stadium captures the Dead in full swing during a pivotal moment of their early‑’70s evolution. The first set rolls along with tight renditions of Bertha, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a crisp Playing In The Band that hints at the fireworks to come. Set two keeps the momentum with Row Jimmy, Here Comes Sunshine and a soulful Jack Straw before handing the reins over to an unforgettable third set. A blistering Truckin' segues into a sprawling Nobody’s Fault But Mine jam, then bursts into a soaring The Other One that many reviewers call “the highlight” of the night. From there the band rides a seamless wave through Eyes Of The World, Stella Blue and Sugar Magnolia, delivering what fans describe as a “cosmic vibe” and a performance where every instrument feels locked in. Jerry’s voice may be hoarse from a cold, but his guitar wails are fiery and inspired; Phil’s bass is pumped and “fired up,” Keith’s keyboards are “great on his game,” and Bill’s drumming provides the driving foundation that keeps the jams propulsive. Donna’s harmonies add an angelic layer throughout. The recording landscape reflects this brilliance – Charlie Miller’s pristine soundboard transfer (gd1973-05-20.sbd.miller.86905.sbeok.flac16) is widely praised as top‑shelf, capturing the full clarity and balance of the night without noticeable flaws, making it the go‑to source for listening.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of the six recordings are flagged as positive and two as mixed, with no negative sentiment. Reviewers consistently praise the explosive third‑set jams (The Other One, Truckin' > Eyes sequence) and highlight Jerry’s passionate playing despite a cold, Keith’s strong keyboards, and Bill’s driving drums. This strong, repeated positivity across multiple sources justifies a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Charlie Miller’s pristine soundboard transfer is described as crystal‑clear, balanced and top‑shelf, capturing the full energy of the show without noticeable glitches.

key_highlights: Explosive third set with a monster The Other One jam
Iconic Truckin' → The Other One → Eyes of the World sequence
Jerry’s fiery guitar work and passionate vocals despite a cold
Keith’s keyboards and Donna’s angelic harmonies add depth

band_performance.Jerry: His raspy, slightly hoarse voice still shines, and his guitar is fiery and tightly locked in throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Fired‑up on bass with a strong presence, especially driving the third‑set jams; a faint buzz noted by one reviewer but overall solid.
band_performance.Keith: Great, on his game – keyboards cut through cleanly and add melodic flair to every segue.
band_performance.Donna: Angelically sweet harmonies that lift the vocal textures, especially on the ballads and segues.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann provides a powerful, driving foundation; reviewers call his drumming ‘right up there’ and essential to the jam flow.
